steps.title: Use the answer denominator as a regression template
steps.content: All choices have denominator $x+1$, so Desmos can fit the expression to the form $\frac{ax^2+bx+c}{x+1}$ using a few valid $x$-values greater than $3$.

steps.title: Read the numerator and confirm the factor cancellation
steps.content:
The regression gives numerator $x^2+3x$, which factors as $x(x+3)$. Algebraically,
$$x^3-9x=x(x-3)(x+3)$$
and
$$x^2-2x-3=(x-3)(x+1)$$
Since $x>3$, $x-3\ne0$, so the common factor cancels.

steps.title: Match the simplified expression
steps.content:
After canceling, the expression is
$$\frac{x(x+3)}{x+1}$$
That matches choice D.
